root_cause: Playwright login helper timing out — auth API works (curl verified), session endpoint returns valid user, but browser-based login via helpers.ts::login() fails to redirect from /login after Sign In button click. Multiple tests blocked at beforeEach login step.

evidence.error_pattern: expect(page).not.toHaveURL(/\/login/) failed — stuck on login page with returnUrl parameter

hypothesis: Better Auth client-side session hydration timing issue in deployed environment. The auth middleware redirects to /login?returnUrl=/ but the Sign In button click doesn't trigger proper redirect. Possible causes: (1) client bundle hydration delay, (2) SameSite cookie attribute mismatch between curl and browser, (3) session token not being read by Next.js middleware in Playwright browser context.
suggested_fix:
1. Increase login timeout in helpers.ts from 20s to 45s
2. Add explicit wait for auth cookie to be set before expecting redirect
3. Investigate Better Auth session middleware timing — may need to add waitForResponse on auth check endpoint
4. Alternative: Use API-based auth (set cookie directly via page.context().addCookies()) instead of UI-based login
notes: Cannot verify v192-fix (BUG-192-001 InlineAiPanel patch) because E2E suite is blocked at auth step. The deployed app's auth system is confirmed working via API testing. This is a test infrastructure issue, NOT a product regression from the fix. Previous v192 E2E run (pipeline-evidence/e2e-v192.json) passed with 95/113 — auth was working then.
verdict: BLOCKED — E2E tests cannot run. Requires test infrastructure fix before v192-fix can be verified.
